stories.title: Cyprus to rebuff calls from Northern frugals to slash EU budget
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Cyprus’ Council presidency is set to turn down Germany’s demands to slash the EU’s next seven-year budget , four EU diplomats told POLITICO. Nicosia, which is set to unveil new budget figures by June 10, is resisting pressure from the EU’s wealthier countries to cut spending on farmers’ subsidies and payments to poorer regions — a move that would irk frugal Northern EU members. However, if the plan goes ahead, it would be a win for Southern and Eastern European countries, w

stories.title: ‘Extremist’ Israeli ministers face EU sanctions push
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — EU countries will be asked to support sanctions on Israeli politicians accused of breaching the rights of foreign activists detained as part of a flotilla that attempted to reach Gaza. Capitals are set to consider imposing the restrictions on senior members of Israel’s government for the first time ahead of a meeting of leaders at the European Council on June 18-19, according to a draft document worked up by officials and obtained by POLITICO . The proposals will be discussed by ambas

stories.title: Cyclist praised for rescuing four people who nearly drowned in Ruhr, Germany
stories.summary: Family were enjoying a waterside barbecue in Bochum before members ended up in jeopardy in river A cyclist has been praised for having “stepped in decisively” and rescuing four members of a family who nearly drowned in the Ruhr River in Germany during a waterside barbecue that almost ended in tragedy. The family of eight had set themselves up on the riverbank in the western city of Bochum, the local fire brigade reported, but the gathering took a panicked turn when one woman got too close to the

stories.title: CBS News veterans urge Paramount CEO to ‘uphold editorial independence’ at 60 Minutes
stories.summary: Dozens of former news staffers press David Ellison to commit to fair coverage in wake of major firings on the show Several dozen veterans of CBS News – including many former 60 Minutes employees – signed a letter to the Paramount Skydance CEO, David Ellison, on Monday, pressing him to commit to the show’s editorial independence four days after network management fired several top staffers and correspondents . On Thursday, the CBS News editor-in-chief, Bari Weiss, and president, Tom Cibrowski, ou

stories.title: Hungary's PM Peter Magyar moves to oust president
stories.summary: Hungary's new Prime Minister, Peter Magyar, has moved to oust the president, threatening constitutional action. Head of state Tamas Sulyok has refused Magyar's demand that he resign.

stories.title: Hungarian PM threatens to oust Orbán-era president
stories.summary: Hungary's president has refused Prime Minister Péter Magyar's demands to step aside, setting up a constitutional clash.

stories.title: Lithuania turns pink for giant cold soup festival
stories.summary: More than 100,000 people are expected to attend Vilnius’s Pink Soup Festival, a three-day celebration of Lithuania’s famous cold beet soup featuring races, costumes, foam slides and mass communal lunches.

stories.title: Poland's controversial 'Highway to Hel' 666 bus revived
stories.summary: Poland's seaside resort called Hel is getting its 666 bus service back, despite earlier opposition from Christian groups.

stories.title: Magyar Péter váratlan ajándéka, egymásra mutogatás és a katázás ígérete – ez történt a parlament hétfői ülésén
stories.summary: Magyar Péter napirend előtt összesen több mint egy órán át beszélt, újra hosszan kiosztotta az ellenzéki pártokat, és váratlan ajándékot adott Toroczkai Lászlónak. Az interpellációk alatt parázs viták alakultak ki, Hegedűs Zsolt vastapsot kapott. Közvetítésünk a parlament hétfői üléséről.

stories.title: Kövesdi Veronika: Sulyok céltáblává tudott válni, jelenléte összesűríti a még itt lévő rendszermaradványokat
stories.summary: A médiakutató arról is beszélt a Telex 2026 című műsorában, hogy Magyar Péternek két perszónája is párhuzamosan épül: a vitázó-gúnyos és a szakértői.

stories.title: Sulyok Tamás szerint az politikai zsákmányszerzés, ha személyre szabott jogalkotással akarják eltávolítani
stories.summary: A köztársasági elnök alkotmányos válsághelyzetről írt, mely károsítja a magyar demokrácia nemzetközi megítélését, és „hatással lehet pl. a jogállamisági feltételekhez kötött uniós források lehívására is”.

stories.title: A koncert előtt rosszul lett a zenekar billentyűse, a közönségből ugrottak be zongorázni
stories.summary: Az egyik dal legbonyolultabb részénél végül improvizált a fiatal beugrós, de így is hatalmas sikert aratott.

stories.summary: Több korábbi bizottsági tag államtitkár lett, ezért veszik át a helyüket más képviselők.

stories.summary: A világsztár repper kétszer is fellépett volna a Szigeten: 2020-ban a Covid, 2025-ben pedig lemondás miatt maradt el a koncertje.

stories.title: Milliárdos bevételt ért el tavaly Orbán Viktor közvetlen munkatársának cége
stories.summary: 30 milliós osztalékot vehet ki Kaminski Fanny.

stories.title: Közel 25 milliárdos osztalékjövedelemmel zárta Orbán Viktor kormányzását a családja
stories.summary: Ráadásul ezt Tiborcz István bármelyik pillanatban megháromszorozhatná, ha kifizetné a BDPST-ben felhalmozott profitot.

stories.summary: Orbán Dávid, a volt miniszterelnök öccse elég jól járt az üzlettel.

stories.summary: A válaszadók 68 százaléka biztosítaná a házasságkötés és az örökbefogadás lehetőségét az azonos nemű pároknak.

stories.title: 20 millió forintnyi büntetést kapott egy teniszező a Roland Garroson, mert azt mondta, nem kéne a nőknek bírónak lenni
stories.summary: Adolfo Daniel Vallejo a második fordulóban esett ki a francia bajnokságon, így a pénzdíja a büntetésének több mint a duplája lett.

stories.summary: A Karmelita és a minisztériumi túrák után a Nemzeti Bank székházában videózott a miniszterelnök.

stories.summary: Borbély vezetése alatt a Győri ETO idén megnyerte a magyar focibajnokságot.

stories.summary: Hiába mondta be a pilóta, hogy ez nagyon nem vicces, és a protokoll szerint a hatóságoknak biztonsági ellenőrzést kell tartani.

stories.title: Egyszer csak a fejemen landolt egy kerti bútor – ilyen volt Travis Scott budapesti bulija
stories.summary: Van, akinek minden pénzt megért, más vegyes érzésekkel távozott Travis Scott káoszos csepeli bulijáról. A helyszínen a fanatikus rajongók mellett T. Danny, Manuel és a helikopterrel bereptetett Beton.Hofi is nyilatkozott a Telexnek.

stories.title: Orbán Anita először a holland külügyminisztert fogadta hivatalos látogatáson
stories.summary: A legfontosabb téma az Erasmus-ösztöndíjak voltak.

stories.title: Júliusban érkezik Charli XCX új albuma, aminek a borítóján Martin Scorsese is befigyel

stories.title: A kétharmados többség bármit megtehet – kreatív jogászkodással távolíthatják el a köztársasági elnököt
stories.summary: Sulyok Tamás nem mondott le a határidőig, és a miniszterelnök bejelentette: Alaptörvény-módosítással mozdítják el a pozíciójából, de hogy hogyan, azt egyelőre a frakciójának se árulta el. Az Alaptörvény módosítása felett nincs tartalmi kontroll, így a tiszások szinte bármit beleírhatnak a szövegbe.

stories.title: Távozik Szentkirályi Alexandra a Fidesz budapesti szervezetének éléről
stories.summary: Az új budapesti elnök Szatmáry Kristóf lesz, aki a választáson 30 százalékponttal kapott ki a választókerületében.

stories.title: Oroszi Beatrix epidemiológus lett az új országos tisztifőorvos
stories.summary: Az új kormány célja a profiltisztítás, kiveszik a gyógyszerfelügyeletet a népegészségügyi központból.

stories.title: What Hungary must do to receive EU funds frozen under Orban
stories.summary: Prime Minister Peter Magyar and European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen announced a deal that could free up over €16 billion in EU funds for Hungary. Now, Magyar's new government needs to deliver.

stories.title: 'I will sleep with fear': Romanians shaken after block of flats hit by drone
stories.summary: A Russian drone hit a block of flats in Romania on Friday, causing a fire and injuring two people, Romanian officials say.

stories.title: Romania: First footage shows damage after drone hits residential block near Ukraine border
stories.summary: A Russian drone from strikes on Ukraine crashes in Galati, injuring two and prompting Romania to seek faster NATO anti-drone support for its air defences.

stories.title: Euronews on the scene after Russian drone strike in Romania
stories.summary: Euronews reports from the Romanian city of Galati, where a Russian drone struck an apartment building near the Ukrainian border. The impact sparked a fire, injured two people and prompted an overnight emergency response.

stories.title: Could Nato be dragged into Ukraine-Russia war? – The Latest
stories.summary: Nato has vowed to ‘defend every inch’ of its territory after a Russian drone hit an apartment building in Romania. The strike prompted swift condemnation and threats of repercussion from European leaders. So is there a risk the war could expand beyond Ukraine? Lucy Hough speaks to senior international correspondent Peter Beaumont Continue reading...

stories.title: Moment Russian drone hits apartment block in Romania
stories.summary: Video captured the moment a Russian Geran-2 drone crashed into an apartment building in eastern Romania near the Ukrainian border. The impact sparked a fire and injured two people, according to Romanian authorities.

stories.title: Odesa and Zaporizhzhia struck as Ukraine faces huge drone barrage
stories.summary: Mass Russian drone strike targets Ukraine as 232 drones launched, 217 downed; Odesa and Zaporizhzhia suffer damage and injuries, ships hit in Black Sea.

stories.title: Alarm at Mexico bill allowing elections to be annulled for ‘foreign interference’
stories.summary: Opposition says constitutional amendment would give bill ruling party carte blanche to overturn will of voters Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email Amid fierce criticism from opposition groups, Mexico ’s senate has passed ‌a constitutional amendment to include “foreign interference” as grounds to annul election results in the country. The bill, which was presented by the country’s president, Claudia Sheinbaum, defines foreign interference as “illicit financing, propaganda, the syste

stories.title: EU unblocks €16 billion in Hungary assistance as Magyar promises reforms
stories.summary: Reforms under new Prime Minister Peter Magyar will enable the EU to free up billions in frozen assistance for Budapest. The EU's Ursula von der Leyen announced the news as Magyar visited Brussels.

stories.title: Romania's political crisis deepens
stories.summary: After Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan’s government fell in early May, Romania's moderate parties have struggled to chart a new course — opening the door for the far right to gain ground.

stories.title: UK will not have to pay Rwanda £100m over failed asylum scheme, court rules
stories.summary: Rwanda had sued UK government over alleged breach of agreement, after scheme scrapped by Labour on first day in office The UK will not have to pay the Rwandan government millions of pounds over a failed migrant deportation scheme set up by Boris Johnson’s administration, an international court has ruled. The east African nation had sued the current UK government for more than £100m, claiming it was owed after a breach of an agreement. Continue reading...

stories.title: Linda Reynolds repays nearly $9,000 after partner billed taxpayers to visit son in Melbourne
stories.summary: Former Liberal senator initially labelled ruling by parliamentary expenses authority ‘patently ridiculous’, saying visits were necessary Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Former Liberal senator Linda Reynolds has paid back nearly $8,800 in parliamentary expenses after her partner claimed family reunion allowances to visit his son in Melbourne while en route to meet her in Canberra. Reynolds accepted the ruling of t

stories.title: France seizes Russian suspected shadow fleet vessel
stories.summary: French authorities on Monday confirmed the country’s Navy intercepted and seized a Russian oil tanker sailing under a false flag. The Tagor, which departed from the Russian port of Murmansk, was boarded more than 400 nautical miles off France’s Atlantic coast on Sunday. French officials suspect the ship was flying a false flag and is part of the so-called shadow fleet of mostly aging oil tankers used to circumvent sanctions imposed after Moscow’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 202

stories.title: Albanian authorities probe seaside resort project linked to Jared Kushner
stories.summary: Albanian anti-corruption prosecutors are investigating a luxury resort project linked to U.S. President Donald Trump’s son-in-law, Jared Kushner, as protests from citizens and environmental organizations over the development persist. The project includes the uninhabited Adriatic island of Sazan and several hundred hectares of the Vjosa-Narta protected landscape, a sensitive coastal wetland area home to flamingos, seals and sea turtle nesting sites. SPAK, Albania’s special anti-corruption p

stories.title: EU negotiators agree new migrant return law
stories.summary: Negotiators on Monday agreed new rules to speed up and increase deportations from the EU, including making it possible to send failed asylum seekers to hubs outside the bloc. The text is part of sweeping reforms the EU is rolling out to increase control over who crosses its external borders and to support countries that receive the most external migrants, with Monday’s agreement landing just days before other migration and asylum reforms start applying on June 12. Monday’s agreement

stories.title: University of Manchester to investigate sexual harassment of female medical students
stories.summary: About 20 students report anonymous late-night phone calls from men who ‘intimidated, demeaned and belittled’ them The University of Manchester has launched an investigation after about 20 female medical students complained of receiving anonymous phone calls in the middle of the night from male callers who intimidated and sexually harassed them. The calls have been going on for at least three years, according to Charlotte Buttercase, a final-year medical student and one of those targeted. Continu

stories.title: Transgender troops can remain in US military, but enlistment can be blocked, court rules
stories.summary: Split decision deals blow to Trump administration’s anti-diversity agenda, calling the ban ‘arbitrary, and based on animus’ Transgender troops can remain in the US military, but the armed services can continue to block their enlistment, an appeals court ruled on Monday in a split decision with potentially significant consequences for the Trump administration’s anti-diversity agenda. The divided, majority opinion by a three-judge panel of the US court of appeals for Washington DC is expected to b

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: Lebanon’s US embassy says Hezbollah has agreed to ‘reciprocal’ ceasefire with Israel under US proposal
stories.summary: Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu has meanwhile threatened to strike Beirut and says IDF will continue operations in southern Lebanon Iran suspends all peace talks in protest at Israeli offensive in Lebanon The exchange of strikes between the US and Iran reflects the fragility of the current ceasefire, which has seen repeated violations even as American and Iranian officials try to negotiate a deal to extend it. Iran has maintained its chokehold on the strait of Hormuz, disrupting global

stories.title: UK wins court case over canceled plan to deport asylum seekers to Rwanda
stories.summary: An international court has rejected a Rwandan claim for millions in damages over a scrapped deal to send asylum seekers to the East African nation.

stories.title: Nigeria police warn against reprisal attacks against South Africans
stories.summary: Tensions have been rising in South Africa in recent weeks following several anti-migrant protests.

stories.title: France seized sanctioned Russian oil tanker with UK help, Macron says
stories.summary: A British helicopter provided support during the operation on Sunday, the UK ministry of defence says.

stories.title: UN urges calm as Israel advances into Lebanon and Iran threatens counter-fronts
stories.summary: The UN has called on all sides to respect the ceasefire following an Israeli military push into Lebanon, which threatens to derail US-Iran peace talks. Israel ordered strikes on Beirut's southern suburbs as Hezbollah targeted Haifa, prompting Iran to threaten the opening of new fronts.

stories.title: Anthropic invites EU to access Mythos hacking tech
stories.summary: Anthropic has extended an invitation to the European Commission granting the EU’s cyber agency access to its powerful AI hacking tool Mythos, according to a Commission official familiar with the process. The AI firm made the formal invitation after a meeting with the Commission in San Francisco last Thursday, the official said, adding the EU now has to put in place a mechanism to access the model with proper security safeguards. Bloomberg reported on Monday that the EU’s Athens-based cybersecuri

stories.title: Top UK minister’s Mandelson messages missing after second reported phone theft
stories.summary: LONDON — Messages between Peter Mandelson and a second high-ranking British government figure are missing from the public disclosures forced by the Jeffrey Epstein scandal because of another reported phone theft, according to documents seen by POLITICO. Nick Thomas-Symonds, the Cabinet minister overseeing the U.K.’s “reset” in relations with the EU, held a formal meeting with Mandelson in January last year about how the then-ambassador might support the ambition from D.C., official disclosures p

stories.title: Colombia’s far-right presidential candidate De la Espriella wins first round of vote ahead of runoff
stories.summary: Lawyer and Trump admirer has risen rapidly in the polls and will face Iván Cepeda in election runoff in three weeks A far-right lawyer and Donald Trump admirer will go head-to-head against leftwing senator Iván Cepeda in the race to be Colombia’s next president after he won a surprise victory in the first round of voting. With 100% of ballots counted, the outsider Abelardo de la Espriella secured 43.7% of the vote – just over 10.3m votes – compared with 40.9% (about 9.6m votes) for Cepeda, a phi

stories.title: French navy seizes Russia-linked oil tanker in Atlantic
stories.summary: President Macron says ship subject to sanctions and posts video of operation that took place with UK support A suspected Russian oil tanker has been detained in the Atlantic, France has announced, in the latest seizure aimed at combatting Moscow’s “shadow fleet” of vessels contravening international sanctions. The Tagor was detained on Sunday morning in international waters more than 400 nautical miles (740km) west of Brittany with the help of the UK and other partners, said the French president

stories.title: French navy boards Russia-linked oil tanker in Atlantic – video
stories.summary: France has released footage that appears to show French naval commandos boarding a ship suspected of being part of Russia's 'shadow fleet' and subject to international sanctions. The French president, Emmanuel Macron, said the operation took place on the tanker, named the Tagor, with support of the UK and in strict compliance with the law of the sea. stories.title: Trump admirer’s surprise first-round win is a blow to Colombia’s traditional conservatives
stories.summary: Success of far-right presidential candidate, Abelardo de la Espriella, suggests some voters are ‘fed up with politics’ The far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella and the leftwing senator Iván Cepeda have just under three weeks to compete for the roughly 3.6m votes that did not go to either of them in the first round of Colombia’s presidential election. That is no insignificant number, given that De la Espriella’s lead over Cepeda amounted to little more than 670,000 votes – 43.7% to 40.9%. Co

stories.summary: Tehran says it holds Israel and US responsible, while Trump says ‘going silent’ on negotiations ‘would be very good’ Middle East live – latest updates Iran has indicated it will suspend peace talks with the US in protest against Israel’s offensive in Lebanon, threatening the collapse of negotiations with Washington as the two sides skirmished amid a faltering ceasefire. The Iranian foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, said: “The ceasefire between Iran and the US is unequivocally a ceasefire on all

stories.title: Trump says Hezbollah and Israel have agreed to ‘stop all shooting’
stories.summary: Declaration comes after Iran had pulled out of peace talks in protest at Israeli offensive in Lebanon • Middle East crisis – live updates Donald Trump has said Hezbollah and Israel have agreed to mutual de-escalation and to scale back fighting, seemingly averting an Israeli strike on Beirut and the potential collapse of ceasefire talks with Iran. The US president said in a social media post that he spoke to Israel’s prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, and representatives of Hezbollah and both ag

stories.title: Labor to highlight ‘risk’ of One Nation to cost of living in bid to dent Pauline Hanson’s popularity
stories.summary: Labor MP Mike Freelander says party must continue to help under-pressure workers, including considering further income tax cuts Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Labor will seek to highlight Pauline Hanson’s record of opposing cost-of-living relief for working people as it tries to prevent One Nation further eroding its voter base. As the government grapples with how to deal with Hanson’s surge, one Labor MP predicted One Nation’s support had peaked and the rightwing p

stories.title: Germany news: Migration to Germany falls sharply in 2025
stories.summary: Fewer migrants are making their way to Germany, with net migration dropping to nearly half what is was. Meanwhile, a court in Düsseldorf has sentenced a man to life in prison over an Islamist attack. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Mozambique: Are 'death squads' targeting the opposition?
stories.summary: Deadly violence against opposition politicians has shaken Mozambique. While the government has called the incidents isolated cases, opposition and civil society warn of a pattern of politically motivated assassinations.

stories.title: Refugees and migration: Is Europe closing its doors?
stories.summary: Migration researchers have called on European governments to abandon isolationist policies. But many politicians are not heeding their calls.

stories.title: US and Iran send mixed messages about plans to pursue a ceasefire agreement. Follow live.
stories.summary: Iranian state media reported that Iran was halting indirect talks with the US after Israel ordered its troops to push deeper into Lebanon. US President Donald Trump said talks were ongoing at a "rapid pace."

stories.title: French Navy boards Russia-linked tanker in Atlantic Ocean
stories.summary: Video released by France shows naval forces boarding the Russia-linked tanker Tagor in the Atlantic Ocean. Paris says the vessel was intercepted as part of efforts to enforce sanctions and curb Russian oil exports funding the war in Ukraine.

stories.title: Kenya protesters oppose Ebola quarantine centre
stories.summary: Hundreds protested in Nanyuki against a planned Ebola quarantine centre as Kenya's High Court suspended the project pending review.

stories.title: Tehran suspended negotiations via mediators with US, Iranian media says
stories.summary: Iran has suspended all indirect talks with the US and announced it would pursue "complete closure of Strait of Hormuz," accusing Israel of "continuing crimes" in Lebanon and stating that any ceasefire breach on one front breaks the truce in general.

stories.title: Driver with no right hand ticketed for using phone in her right hand
stories.summary: Bodycam video from Florida shows a police officer issuing a texting-while-driving citation to a woman, despite her not having a right hand. The ticket has since been recommended for dismissal, according to US media reports.

stories.title: France bans Israel from major weapons show
stories.summary: PARIS — France has banned Israel from participating in Eurosatory, one of the world’s largest arms shows that kicks off later this month, the Israeli government announced on Monday. “The French decision encompasses: a ban on government representatives attending the exhibition; a ban on opening an Israeli national pavilion; and a restriction limiting Israeli defense industries to displaying air defense products only, with offensive systems explicitly excluded,” the Israeli

stories.title: Fuck the Polis review – cryptic docu-essay is a sphinxlike study of Greek myth and modernity
stories.summary: This film may be making a point about the classical vis a vis the contemporary, but its visual collages and dense poetic texts render it inert The title of this lyrical but frustrating docu-essay about director Rita Azevedo Gomes’s travels in Greece cuts both ways. Is it expressing impatience with the classical ideals she hopes to discover there; or, borrowed from street graffiti, is it actually critiquing the modern society that has betrayed ancient standards of beauty and harmony and, in the w

stories.title: ‘What happened to the testicles?’: mockery in Milan over bull mosaic’s restoration
stories.summary: Rampant Bull needed a makeover after wear and tear from tourists, but refurbishment ‘castrated’ it, critics say The restoration of a floor mosaic in Milan called the Rampant Bull has been mocked after the works appear to have erased a crucial anatomical detail – its testicles. The 19th-century mosaic in the Galleria Vittorio Emanuele II shopping arcade needed a makeover because a small crater had formed in the tiny pink tiles featuring the bull’s testicles, due to the constant stream of tourists

stories.title: People ‘panicking’ as Ghana passes sweeping law criminalising LGBTQ+ activity
stories.summary: Community groups say some fear they could lose homes, jobs and access to healthcare if the new law is ratified by President John Dramani Mahama Ghana’s LGBTQ community is living in fear after the country’s parliament approved a sweeping bill that criminalises the promotion of LGBTQ+ activities and identifying as lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender or queer, rights groups have warned. The legislation, which was passed on Friday, mandates prison sentences of three to 10 years. Continue reading...

stories.title: Australia’s 178 billionaires are $25.7bn richer than last year as 3.7 million live in poverty
stories.summary: Oxfam finds the 20 richest Australians now hold more wealth than the bottom 3 million households Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The wealth of Australia’s billionaires increased by $25.67bn in the past year, equivalent to almost $50,000 a minute, according to new Oxfam Australia analysis of the 2026 Australian Financial Review Rich List. stories.title: Property prices may drop but it’s decades of policy failure, not the budget, to blame
stories.summary: Instead of relying on tax-enhanced speculation, investors must now look at established properties based on actual profitability – leaving space for first home buyers Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast For decades, Australia’s property market has been defined by relentless price rises, reinforcing the old adage that real estate investment is “as safe as houses”. There’s now a wrinkle in that wisdom. Continue reading.

stories.summary: Conquering of Beaufort Castle for first time in 26 years brings back memories of occupation of south Middle East crisis – live updates When Hussain Alawieh used to take tourists to Beaufort Castle, they would marvel at the view. The ancient hilltop fort, captured nearly 1,000 years earlier by Crusaders, still offered the same sweeping panoramic views of south Lebanon and the Litani River that empires fought over for a millennia. On Sunday, the view from the castle was obscured by white phosphoru

stories.title: EasyJet is an obvious takeover target, but US approach may not be a flyer | Nils Pratley
stories.summary: Castlelake’s move raises questions over valuation and ownership rules as well as whether Stelios Haji-Ioannou could throw a spanner in the works EasyJet says US takeover bid would be ‘highly opportunistic’ A share price gain of only 10% on a possible takeover approach is a meek reaction. If the stock market truly believed that Castlelake, a US investment fund, stood a decent chance of buying easyJet , you would expect the target’s stock to fly significantly higher. stories.title: Explosion at Malta fireworks factory sends plumes of smoke into air – video

stories.summary: Ballots are being cast in the first round of the South American nation’s presidential elections Colombians are casting ballots in the first round of the South American nation’s presidential election, choosing between candidates with radically diverging visions for the future of peace in a country haunted by decades of armed conflict. The vote on Sunday, seen as a referendum on outgoing President Gustavo Petro’s policies, comes 10 years after Colombia signed a historic peace pact with guerrillas

stories.title: Clashes in Paris after PSG win Champions League final – video
stories.summary: French police clashed with rioters in the capital after Paris Saint-Germain defeated Arsenal to win the Champions League title . Hundreds of people were detained across France, with the majority in the Paris area French police arrest 780 after violent clashes as PSG fans celebrate Champions League win Continue reading...

stories.title: Japan defence minister denies militarism and criticises China's 'huge arsenal'
stories.summary: Shinjiro Koizumi makes some of Tokyo's most pointed remarks yet in the dispute over Japan's military build-up.

stories.title: Hundreds arrested and dozens of police injured after Champions League riots in France
stories.summary: Nearly 800 people were arrested after clashes with police which saw 219 people injured, including 57 police officers.

stories.title: Colombia prepares to go to polls in election shadowed by resurgence of political violence
stories.summary: Sunday’s presidential vote is contest between left and right – and between contradictory proposals for dealing with the decades-long armed conflict Mateo Pérez Rueda was one internship away from completing a degree in political science. The 24-year-old also worked as a bicycle delivery rider and sold fruit salads and juice to finance his passion: the Colombian independent digital magazine El Confidente . On 4 May he travelled to Briceño, in the western province of Antioquia, to report on the lon

stories.title: Chinese dissident says he was berated by ‘pro-regime’ interpreter for UK police
stories.summary: Hong Qi, who orchestrated protest against Communist government, claims interpreter on 101 call launched political tirade A Chinese dissident who orchestrated an anti-government protest in China after fleeing to the UK has claimed that a “pro-regime” interpreter used by a British police force berated him when he sought help. Hong Qi, who made headlines last year after using a mobile phone while in the UK to remotely project anti-regime slogans on to a building in his home city, Chongqing, contact

stories.title: Death of Congolese man renews scrutiny of race relations in Ireland
stories.summary: Yves Sakila died after being restrained by security guards ‘in broad daylight’ Irish authorities have agreed to a second postmortem on the body of a Congolese man who died after being restrained by shop security guards on a Dublin street, prompting an outcry and comparisons to the death of George Floyd . A forensic pathologist from England is to conduct an independent postmortem this week on Yves Sakila, 35, an alleged shoplifter who was pursued and pinned to the ground in the city centre on 15

stories.title: WHO calls for community cooperation to contain Ebola outbreak in DRC
stories.summary: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus makes appeal after protests against protocols for handling victims’ bodies in Ituri province Containing the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo requires community cooperation and is “everybody’s business”, the World Health Organization has said. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus , the organisation’s director general, made the plea on Sunday during a visit to eastern Congo where some residents have protested against stringent medical protocols for handling victi

stories.title: Goodbye, Norma Jeane: How Marilyn Monroe changed Hollywood
stories.summary: Long seen primarily as a sex symbol, Marilyn Monroe is now viewed as an early advocate for women in Hollywood. On the 100th anniversary of her birth, DW looks at the star who balanced fame and feminist agency.

stories.title: Space race: Why Portugal is reaching for the stars
stories.summary: A spaceport is being built on a small Portuguese island in the Atlantic. Is Portugal on the verge of becoming a space nation?

stories.title: How the Alevi community came to thrive in Germany
stories.summary: The Alevis make up the fourth largest religious community in Germany today. What are the main tenets of their faith? And how are they preserving their unique culture?

stories.title: Ferrari wanted to take on Chinese EVs with the Luce - then the backlash started
stories.summary: The brand's first EV has been heavily criticised, with some saying it has abandoned Ferrari's roots.

stories.title: How Putin became master of the image
stories.summary: From enigmatic KGB agent to wartime ruler, this is how Putin has repeatedly reinvented his image, and himself.

stories.title: Cuba’s blackouts leave high-rise residents with constant uncertainty
stories.summary: Will Grant spoke to a 70-year-old widow who says the inability to use her building's elevator during a power outage trapped her and her husband when he needed medical care.

stories.title: Pashinyan on course for landslide victory and pro-West mandate in Armenia election, new poll shows
stories.summary: A new poll projects Armenian Premier Nikol Pashinyan’s party could win a large parliamentary majority, as Russia intensifies its campaign to counter Yerevan's pro-West realignment, warning of a Ukraine scenario.

stories.title: French police detain hundreds in Paris after violent celebrations of PSG’s Champions League title
stories.summary: The French interior ministry said that some 780 persons were questioned nationally, including 283 in Paris alone.

stories.title: Israel retakes Beaufort castle in Lebanon, Paris issues strong condemnation
stories.summary: Israeli forces push deeper into Lebanon and raise their flag over Beaufort Castle, a strategic fortress. France condemns the move and demands a UN Security Council meeting.

stories.title: Laos cave survivors help with plan to find last two missing men
stories.summary: So far, five men have been freed from the narrow, flooded cave but efforts are ongoing to locate two more.

stories.title: South Africa made to look like fools over World Cup visa issues, says minister
stories.summary: After a day of uncertainty, the government confirms the team will depart for Mexico on Monday.

stories.title: 'Cancel it', Trump says after artists drop out of US Freedom 250 festival
stories.summary: Trump suggests holding a "Make America Great Again" rally instead after most headliners drop out of White-House-linked event.

stories.title: Dead whale towed ashore in Denmark ahead of autopsy
stories.summary: "Timmy" was found dead off the coast of Anholt despite multiple attempts to rescue it.

stories.title: Brazil monitors two patients for possible Ebola infection
stories.summary: If confirmed, they would be the first infection cases outside Africa, since the outbreak began in DR Congo.

stories.title: Between celebration and confrontation: Paris after PSG victory
stories.summary: Nearly 800 people were arrested after clashes with police following the Champions League final.

stories.title: Exam fail: Indian students complain en masse about marking errors in key final exams
stories.summary: New digital marking system is aimed at reducing human errors but many students say it has resulted in wrong grades A national outcry has erupted in India after more than 400,000 students requested copies of their answer sheets amid mounting complaints of errors in the marking of the country’s most important school-leaving examinations. Within days of the grade 12 exam results being issued, students began reporting marking discrepancies they linked to a new digital marking system. Continue readin

stories.title: Afghan refugee truck crash kills 18, injures 35 in eastern Afghanistan
stories.summary: Zabihullah Mujahid, the spokesperson for the Taliban government, expressed deep sorrow over the tragedy, which took place at the conclusion of the Muslim holiday of Eid al-Adha, and offered condolences to the families of the victims

stories.title: UK, US and Australia to develop 'cutting-edge' underwater drone technology
stories.summary: The new technology is expected to be ready by 2027 and is designed to help defend critical subsea cables and pipelines.

stories.title: Trump's doctor says he's in excellent health - but are US presidents' health checks a PR exercise?
stories.summary: Every president in modern history - including Donald Trump this week - has taken an annual physical exam that is as much about messaging as it is about health.

stories.title: Eighteen people killed in Afghanistan truck crash, including 10 children
stories.summary: Truck was carrying Afghan families returning Pakistan when it overturned, official says A truck overturned in eastern Afghanistan on Saturday, killing 18 people on board including 10 children, a provincial official told Agence France-Presse. Deadly traffic crashes are common in Afghanistan, due in part to poor roads after decades of conflict, dangerous driving and a lack of regulation. Continue reading...

stories.title: Four more men freed from flooded Laos cave in hazardous rescue mission
stories.summary: Two still missing as divers make their way deeper into cave through muddy water and sharp rocks to find them Four more miners who were trapped in a flooded cave in Laos for 10 days have been freed by divers, but two people are still missing as rescuers continue to crawl through narrow, deluged tunnels and sharp rocks to find them. The first of the party of seven men was rescued on Friday in a perilous rescue mission which has required teams to drain water from the cave and navigate collapse haza

stories.title: International aid cuts complicate Congo's Ebola epidemic
stories.summary: The scaling back of development aid has contributed to the inability to contain the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo. German organizations are calling for a major spending boost to help stop the spread.

stories.title: Malta votes in snap parliamentary election
stories.summary: Voting is underway in Malta's parliamentary election. Opinion polls show Prime Minister Robert Abela on course for reelection in the shadow of the Middle East crisis.
